claim: Use the PIV Loop (Plan, Implement, Validate) for per-ticket development cycles to keep your agent focused and context clean
actionable: When picking up a new ticket, structure agent work in three explicit phases: Plan the implementation approach, delegate code generation, then validate output against acceptance criteria.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer

claim: Start each coding session with a 'prime' command that loads external context (Jira tickets, Confluence pages, git history) to help the agent understand the codebase before implementation
actionable: Create a reusable prime command that pulls Jira issue context, recent git commits, and architecture documentation before starting implementation work.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer

claim: Separate planning from implementation into different Claude Code sessions to avoid agent bias and ensure fresh perspective during coding
actionable: After creating a detailed implementation plan markdown, open a new blank Claude Code session and pass only the plan file to avoid context carryover that biases the implementation.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer

claim: Turn any prompt you use more than 3 times into a reusable command or skill to standardize AI workflows across your team
actionable: When you find yourself repeating a prompt pattern, extract it into your .claude/commands or skills folder and invoke via slash notation.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer

claim: Use System Evolution to improve your AI layer (rules, commands, skills) whenever a coding agent makes a mistake, so the fix applies to future work across the team
actionable: After each bug or mistake from your agent, spend time updating global rules or commands to prevent that class of issue recurring in future tickets.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer

claim: Have the coding agent ask you clarifying questions one-by-one during planning to reduce downstream assumptions and misalignment before implementation
actionable: In the planning phase, explicitly ask your agent to pose clarifying questions about requirements, architecture, and scope before creating the implementation plan.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer

claim: Encode your team's existing software development practices (PR process, code review, testing strategy) into commands so agents follow your conventions, not force you to adopt new ones
actionable: Review your current SDLC and translate key checkpoints (linting, type checking, test coverage) into the validation section of your implement command.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer

claim: Store PRDs and implementation plans as markdown artifacts that become the single source of truth, avoiding the need to maintain context across multiple sessions
actionable: After planning phase, commit the plan.md to source control and pass it to the implementation agent as the sole input, removing session-dependent context.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer

claim: Use sub-agents for research-heavy tasks (codebase analysis, web research) to avoid overwhelming the main agent with tens of thousands of tokens and get back concise summaries
actionable: When exploring a codebase or researching libraries, spawn 2-3 sub-agents in parallel to investigate independently, then let the main agent synthesize their findings.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer

claim: Have your coding agent automatically run validation (linting, type checking, unit tests, even end-to-end tests with browser automation) before passing control back to you
actionable: Build validation steps into your implement command so the agent runs all tests, formatting, and type checks and reports results before requesting human code review.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer

claim: Treat an over-engineered framework like BMAD or GitHub Spec Kit as a warning: keep your AI coding foundation lightweight so it molds to your existing team practices instead of forcing wholesale process change
actionable: When adopting a new AI workflow framework, audit your current SDLC first and design commands/skills to integrate with it rather than replace it entirely.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer

claim: Use Jira MCP server (or equivalent CI/CD tooling MCPs) to automate ticket creation, branch management, and PR updates so developers don't waste time on administrative work
actionable: Connect the Atlassian MCP server to Claude Code and invoke create_issue commands from your plan to generate tickets without manual Jira navigation.
source_title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer
